title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to hold a pre-draft workout for UConn forward Alex Karaban next week.
Karaban ranks No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects with Celtics holding picks No. 27 and No. 40.
Karaban, a Southborough native, won two NCAA titles at UConn and holds several school records.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth's concert at MGM Music Hall emphasized musicianship over spectacle, combining jazz and pop influences.
Puth incorporated both his new album 'Whatever’s Clever!' and older hits, using fresh arrangements for live performances.
Openers Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey enhanced the show with indie-pop and live looping performances, respectively.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed "Bringing Down the Horse" in full, followed by Tom Petty's "Long After Dark" at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan's gruffer vocals added expressiveness to tracks like "Sixth Avenue Heartache" and "The Difference" during the sold-out show.
The Wallflowers' set included a mix of hits, lesser-known tracks, and Petty covers, ending with an encore featuring "Refugee" and "The Waiting."]}

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, marking the largest decline among Massachusetts cities according to new U.S. Census data.
Everett and Worcester saw the biggest population gains in Massachusetts, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 people in 2025, a 0.2% increase, slower than nearly 1% growth the previous year.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments, controlled by the Johnson family, ranked 14th on Forbes' list of largest family businesses.
Tewksbury-based Demoulas Super Markets, Market Basket's parent company, ranked 69th with $8 billion annual revenue.
Forbes' list includes companies with significant multigenerational family ownership, with Fidelity at 49% and Demoulas at 100%.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major campus housing overhaul.
The project aims to modernize housing, expand amenities, and maintain affordability as enrollment rises.
Redevelopment will be phased to allow students to stay while buildings are renovated or replaced.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at the Chevalier Theatre in Medford on May 22, featuring songs from her new album 'World's Gone Wrong'.
Williams' setlist included protest songs from recent albums and covers of classics like Neil Young's 'Rockin' in the Free World'.
Despite not playing guitar due to a stroke, Williams engaged the audience with her topical lyrics and vocal performances.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox lost 8-6 to the Twins at Fenway Park, with Justin Slaten struggling in relief.
Payton Tolle had nine strikeouts before being replaced after the sixth inning on 85 pitches.
Slaten allowed four hits and four runs in less than an inning, including two home runs.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the first pitch to his son Kody Clemens at Fenway Park before the game against the Twins.
Clemens, a Red Sox legend, wore a custom 'Rocket Man' jersey and signed autographs for fans outside Fenway Park.
The Red Sox distributed Roger Clemens bobbleheads, commemorating his career achievements, during the event at the ballpark.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to the NBA All-Defensive First Team for the 2025-26 season.
White recorded 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading all guards with 552 contested shots.
Celtics finished second in the Eastern Conference with a 56-26 record despite playoff exit.
